items.text:
Notice: Elevator Maintenance — Pine Ridge Residence Hall

From Monday, June 3 through Friday, June 7, the two main elevators in Pine Ridge Residence Hall will be taken out of service for their annual safety inspection. Maintenance will take place each day from 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM, performed by Elevatec Services on behalf of Campus Facilities.

Please note the following:

• The freight elevator at the south end of the building will remain available for residents with mobility needs and may be requested at the front desk
• Residents who request the freight elevator more than three times in one day are asked to pay a $5 daily service fee
• Move-in carts will not be available during the inspection window; please plan deliveries before June 3 or after June 7
• Replacement room keys cannot be issued from the front desk during inspection hours

Residents with accessibility concerns should contact Ms. Suárez at the housing office by Friday, May 31 at pineridge.housing@campus.edu. Routine questions may be sent to the same address after that date. The inspection is required by city code, and we appreciate your patience while the elevators are tested.

questions.stem: According to the notice, which residents may use the freight elevator?

options.A: Residents who arrive at the building before 8:00 AM.
options.B: Residents who pay a one-time access fee at check-in.
options.C: Residents who have completed the safety inspection class.
options.D: Residents with mobility needs who request it at the front desk.

questions.explanation: The notice states that the freight elevator is available for residents with mobility needs and may be requested at the front desk.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a resident who plans to move in heavy boxes on June 4?

options.A: They are likely to receive a key card upgrade from Ms. Suárez.
options.B: They are likely to experience difficulty without move-in carts available.
options.C: They are likely to be reassigned to a different residence hall.
options.D: They are likely to need a new email address to enter the building.

questions.explanation: The notice states that move-in carts will not be available during the inspection window, suggesting moving heavy boxes will be difficult.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To inform residents about a scheduled elevator inspection and its effects.
options.B: To announce the permanent removal of the freight elevator from service.
options.C: To advertise a new maintenance contract with Elevatec Services on campus.
options.D: To require all residents to attend a safety inspection class next month.

questions.explanation: The notice as a whole informs residents about the annual elevator inspection and how it affects building access.

items.text:
Notice: Updated Lost and Found Policy — Maple Library

Starting Monday, September 16, Maple Library will operate under a new lost and found policy. Items found in study rooms, the reading hall, or near the front entrance will be logged at the main service desk on the second floor within one business day. The new policy is intended to streamline retrieval and reduce the number of items left unclaimed at the end of each semester.

Key changes:

• Items will be held for thirty days, after which unclaimed items will be donated to the Eastside community shelter
• High-value items such as laptops, phones, and wallets will be held for sixty days at the main service desk
• Owners must show a current student ID or government-issued photo ID before any item is released
• An online lost-item portal is now available at library.campus.edu/lost — searches are free and submissions take about three minutes to complete

For questions about a specific item, contact Mr. Choi at the main service desk between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM, Monday through Friday. Reports filed before the close of business each Friday will be processed by the following Monday. The library appreciates your help in returning belongings to their owners.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how long are high-value items kept before release?

options.A: They are held for fifteen business days only.
options.B: They are held for thirty days at the desk.
options.C: They are held for sixty days at the desk.
options.D: They are held for ninety days at the desk.

questions.explanation: The notice states that high-value items such as laptops, phones, and wallets will be held for sixty days at the main service desk.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To advertise a new community shelter partnership program.
options.B: To announce that lost and found service has been discontinued.
options.C: To explain a revised lost and found policy at the library.
options.D: To require all students to register a personal item tracker.

questions.explanation: The notice as a whole explains the revised lost and found policy and its key changes.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about an unclaimed jacket left in a study room?

options.A: It is likely to be auctioned at a campus fundraiser.
options.B: It is likely to be donated to a community shelter eventually.
options.C: It is likely to be returned by mail to the owner's address.
options.D: It is likely to be discarded after a single business day.

questions.explanation: The notice states that ordinary items unclaimed after thirty days will be donated to the Eastside community shelter.
